subject: Digital signature security strength from hash function collision resistance
truth: Stop using a hash function weaker than the signature algorithm's key strength; the security strength of a digital signature is no greater than the minimum of the algorithm's key strength and the hash function's collision resistance.
quote: The security strength provided by a digital signature generation process is no greater than the minimum of 1) the security strength that the digital signature algorithm can support with a given key length and any domain parameters used , 2) the security strength (with respect to collision resistance) supported by the cryptographic hash function that is used to hash the data to be signed.

subject: Authorization responses over unencrypted connections
truth: Authorization responses MUST NOT be transmitted over unencrypted network connections; authorization servers MUST NOT allow redirection URIs that use the http scheme except for native clients using loopback interface redirection.
quote: Authorization responses MUST NOT be transmitted over unencrypted network connections

subject: Fallback SCSV for protocol downgrade prevention
truth: If the client does not support TLS 1.3 and is attempting to connect with a TLS version prior to TLS 1.2, the client shall include TLS_FALLBACK_SCSV at the end of the cipher suite list to prevent unintended protocol downgrades.
quote: TLS_FALLBACK_SCSV at the end of the cipher suite list

subject: reauthentication via federation
truth: When requiring reauthentication through a federation protocol, specify the maximum acceptable authentication age to the CSP and the CSP SHALL reauthenticate if the subscriber has not been authenticated within that time period.
quote: specify the maximum acceptable authentication age to the CSP

subject: Authenticator unique authentication to verifier
truth: Stop using out-of-band authenticators that do not uniquely authenticate to the verifier; the authenticator SHALL uniquely authenticate itself via an authenticated protected channel with approved cryptography or a SIM card.
quote: The out-of-band authenticator SHALL uniquely authenticate itself in one of the following ways when communicating with the verifier

subject: Verifier impersonation (phishing) resistance
truth: Do not accept an authentication that does not strongly and irreversibly bind the authenticated protected channel identifier to the authenticator output; validate the signature proving verifier impersonation resistance.
quote: strongly and irreversibly bind a channel identifier that was negotiated in establishing the authenticated protected channel to the authenticator output

subject: Multi-factor authenticator binding requires multi-factor authentication
truth: When binding a new multi-factor authenticator, require multi-factor authentication (or equivalent, such as association with a just-completed identity proofing session) — do not bind using single-factor authentication.
quote: Binding of multi-factor authenticators SHALL require multi-factor authentication or equivalent

subject: Key agreement vs key transport
truth: Do not use key transport when key agreement is required; key agreement ensures neither party can predetermine the shared secret independently, unlike key transport where one party selects the keying material.
quote: Key agreement A (pair-wise) key-establishment procedure where secret keying material is generated from information contributed by two participants so that no party can predetermine the value of the secret keying material independently from the contributions of the other party. Contrast with key-transport.

subject: OTP entropy below 64 bits requires rate-limiting
truth: If the authenticator output or activation secret has less than 64 bits of entropy, the verifier SHALL implement a rate-limiting mechanism that effectively limits the number of failed authentication attempts.
quote: less than 64 bits of entropy, the verifier SHALL implement a rate-limiting mechanism

subject: Authorization server redirect behavior on error/denial
truth: Do not automatically redirect the user agent to the client's redirect_uri when the user denies consent or when the request is erroneous; authenticate the user first and only redirect if the URI is trusted.
quote: authorization server MUST always authenticate the user first

subject: DH finite-field parameters (1024, 160)
truth: Stop using DH finite-field parameters with less than 112 bits of security strength, specifically (len(p), len(q)) = (1024, 160) is disallowed; use safe-prime groups or (2048, 224) or (2048, 256) instead.
quote: (len(p), len(q)) = (1024, 160) Disallowed

subject: Server must verify PSK-associated parameters match for 0-RTT
truth: To accept early data, the server MUST verify that TLS version, cipher suite, and ALPN protocol match those associated with the selected PSK; if any check fails, the server MUST NOT accept early data.
quote: MUST verify that the following values are the same as those associated with the selected PSK

subject: CBC mode cipher suites in TLS < 1.3
truth: When supporting TLS versions prior to 1.3 with CBC mode cipher suites, use the bad_record_mac error for padding errors and compute the MAC regardless of padding errors, with constant-time decryption.
quote: compute the MAC regardless of whether padding errors exist

subject: Manual-entry authenticators (OOB, OTP) are NOT verifier impersonation-resistant
truth: Stop treating manually-entered OTP or out-of-band authenticator outputs as resistant to verifier impersonation; they do not bind the output to the specific session and are vulnerable to MitM replay.
quote: SHALL NOT be considered verifier impersonation-resistant because the manual entry does not bind the authenticator output to the specific session

subject: HTTPS does not protect cookies from network attacker injection via HTTP
truth: Do not assume HTTPS-only cookies are safe from injection; an active network attacker can inject Set-Cookie headers by impersonating an HTTP response, and the HTTPS server cannot distinguish them.
quote: An active network attacker can also inject cookies into the Cookie header sent to https://example.com/ by impersonating a response from http://example.com/ and injecting a Set-Cookie header

subject: TLS 1.3 downgrade protection random bytes
truth: When negotiating TLS 1.2 or below, set the last 8 bytes of ServerHello.random to the downgrade sentinel values (44 4F 57 4E 47 52 44 01 for TLS 1.2, 44 4F 57 4E 47 52 44 00 for TLS 1.1 or below).
quote: set the last 8 bytes of their Random value specially

subject: Encryption-then-authentication order
truth: When combining encryption and authentication as separate operations, use well-vetted standardized protocols and constructions; performing operations in the wrong order introduces vulnerabilities.
quote: If care is not taken in performing these operations (e.g., performing the operations in the right order), vulnerabilities can be introduced

associative: separate keys for encryption and authentication

subject: 80-bit security strength is deprecated; 112-bit minimum required
truth: Stop using cryptographic algorithms and key lengths that provide less than 112 bits of security strength; use only algorithms and key lengths that provide at least 112 bits of security strength.
quote: required security strength of at least 112 bits

subject: TLS version negotiation fallback
truth: When the supported_versions extension is absent from ClientHello, a server compliant with RFC 8446 that also supports TLS 1.2 or earlier MUST negotiate using the legacy version-negotiation mechanism (ClientHello.legacy_version), per RFC 8446 §4.2.1 — this is normal, expected backward-compatible fallback for pre-TLS-1.3 clients, not a condition to reject. (A server that supports only TLS 1.3 has no legacy version to fall back to and may simply fail to negotiate/reject such a ClientHello, but that is a consequence of not supporting older versions, not a MUST-NOT-fallback rule.)
quote: When it is absent from the ClientHello message, the server shall use the version negotiation specified in TLS 1.2 and earlier.
_audit_fix: WRONG: The truth directly inverts its own cited quote: RFC 8446 / NIST SP 800-52r2 state that when supported_versions is absent, the server SHALL use the legacy TLS 1.

subject: TLS 1.3 ServerHello key_share group consistency
truth: If using (EC)DHE and a HelloRetryRequest was received, the client MUST verify the ServerHello selected NamedGroup matches the HelloRetryRequest selected_group, or abort with illegal_parameter.
quote: the client MUST verify that the selected NamedGroup in the ServerHello is the same as that in the HelloRetryRequest.

subject: Secret and private key confidentiality
truth: Do not treat secret keys (symmetric) and private keys (asymmetric) as interchangeable; both need protection against unauthorized disclosure, and all keying material needs integrity protection.
quote: algorithms, respectively) and any secret metadata need to be protected against unauthorized

subject: Mixed content loading
truth: Stop loading any resource (CSS, SWF, scripts) over insecure connections; a single insecure embedding anywhere on the site compromises the security of the entire site including the login page.
quote: a single insecure embedding anywhere on the overall site compromises the security of their login page

subject: TLS server certificate Extended Key Usage
truth: Require id-kp-serverAuth in the Extended Key Usage extension for TLS server certificates; prohibit anyExtendedKeyUsage and all other EKU values unless consistent with the key usage extension.
quote: Prohibited: anyExtendedKeyUsage; all others unless consistent with key usage extension

subject: Outdated algorithm/key size guidance
truth: Stop relying on static algorithm recommendations; check SP 800-131A for current approved algorithms and key sizes, as guidance is updated when algorithms no longer provide adequate security.
quote: SP 800-57, Part 1 is updated whenever the guidance provided therein is no longer valid (e.g., an algorithm no longer provides adequate security)

subject: Verifier-CSP communications must use mutually-authenticated secure channel with approved cryptography
truth: Stop using unauthenticated or weakly-authenticated channels between verifier and CSP; use a mutually-authenticated secure channel (e.g., client-authenticated TLS) with approved cryptography.
quote: mutually-authenticated secure channel (such as a client-authenticated TLS connection) using approved cryptography

associative: secure channel between verifier and CSP

subject: Early exporter vs regular exporter misuse
truth: Implementations MUST use the exporter_master_secret unless explicitly specified by the application; a separate interface for the early exporter is RECOMMENDED to prevent accidental misuse.
quote: Implementations MUST use the exporter_master_secret unless explicitly specified by the application

subject: Referer header from secure to cross-origin
truth: A user agent SHOULD NOT send a Referer header field if the referring resource was accessed with a secure protocol and the request target has a different origin, unless explicitly allowed.
quote: A user agent SHOULD NOT send a Referer header field if the referring resource was accessed with a secure protocol and the request target has an origin differing from that of the referring resource

associative: Referer header HTTPS to different origin

subject: Silent authentication (prompt=none) open redirect
truth: Do not automatically redirect the user agent to the attacker-controlled redirect_uri during silent authentication (prompt=none) unless the authorization server trusts the redirection URI.
quote: authorization server SHOULD only automatically redirect the user agent if it trusts the redirection URI

subject: Trailer field merging
truth: Do not merge trailer fields into the header section unless the recipient knows the corresponding field definition permits and defines how to merge; otherwise store separately or discard.
quote: implementations are encouraged either to store the trailer fields separately or to discard them instead of merging

subject: 80-bit protected data processing carries accepted risk
truth: Processing already-protected data at the 80-bit security strength is allowed but a certain amount of risk must be accepted; do not apply new cryptographic protections at 80-bit strength.
quote: processing of this already-protected data at the lower security strength is allowed, but a certain amount of risk must be accepted

subject: EdDSA deterministic signatures vs ECDSA/DSA random nonces
truth: Prefer EdDSA (Ed25519/Ed448) over ECDSA/DSA because EdDSA signatures are deterministic and do not require a random bit generator for each signature, avoiding nonce reuse vulnerabilities.
quote: EdDSA signatures are deterministic: the unique value is computed using the private key and the message to be signed

subject: TLS 1.3 client downgrade check
truth: TLS 1.3 clients receiving a ServerHello indicating TLS 1.2 or below MUST check the last 8 random bytes are not the downgrade sentinel values and abort with illegal_parameter if matched.
quote: MUST check that the last 8 bytes are not equal to either of these values

subject: TLS server cipher suite configuration
truth: Configure the server to only use NIST-recommended cipher suites by disabling all non-approved cipher suites, especially when the server does not allow administrator preference ordering.
quote: the only way to ensure that a server uses NIST-approved algorithms is to disable cipher suites that use other algorithms

subject: Rate-limiting for low-entropy look-up secrets
truth: For look-up secrets that have less than 64 bits of entropy, the verifier SHALL implement a rate-limiting mechanism that effectively limits the number of failed authentication attempts (NIST SP 800-63B-4, §3.1.2.2, final 2025-07-31 — supersedes the withdrawn SP 800-63B).
quote: limiting mechanism that effectively limits the number of failed authentication attempts that can
_audit_fix: STALE: The cited source, NIST SP 800-63B, was officially withdrawn on 2025-08-01 and superseded by SP 800-63B-4 (part of the SP 800-63-4 suite, revised for syncable au

subject: TLS server handling missing client certificate
truth: Configure the TLS server to terminate the connection with a fatal 'handshake failure' alert when a client certificate is requested but the client does not have a suitable certificate.
quote: failure” alert when a client certificate is requested and the client does not have a suitable

subject: reauthentication using session secret alone
truth: A session SHALL NOT be extended past the reauthentication timeout guidelines based on presentation of the session secret alone; reauthentication requires prompting for additional authentication factor(s) per the AAL reauthentication requirements table in NIST SP 800-63B-4, Section 5 (Session Management) — supersedes the withdrawn SP 800-63B, whose equivalent table was numbered 7-1.
quote: A session SHALL NOT be extended past the guidelines
_audit_fix: STALE: Source document NIST SP 800-63B was withdrawn 2025-08-01 and superseded by SP 800-63B-4; the exact table/section numbering for reauthentication rules may have m

subject: Key management as security foundation
truth: Do not assume strong algorithms alone provide security; the protection of data reduces to the protection of key information, requiring a Cryptographic Key Management System (CKMS).
quote: the security of information protected by cryptography directly depends on the protection afforded the key information

subject: TLS 1.3 record layer version field
truth: Set legacy_record_version to 0x0303 for all TLS 1.3 records except initial ClientHello; do not use 0x0300 (SSL 3.0) or 0x0301 (TLS 1.0) except for backward-compatible ClientHello.
quote: MUST be set to 0x0303 for all records generated by a TLS 1.3 implementation

subject: Deprecated symmetric-key algorithms for encryption
truth: Stop using deprecated symmetric-key algorithms for applying cryptographic protection (encryption); they may only be used for processing already-protected information (decryption).
quote: cryptographic protection (e.g., encryption) but may continue to be used for processing

subject: Domain cookie scoping allows sibling-domain overwrite
truth: Do not assume cookies set by one subdomain are isolated from sibling subdomains; a server on foo.example.com can overwrite cookies for example.com that bar.example.com relies on.
quote: Cookies do not provide integrity guarantees for sibling domains

subject: legacy_compression_methods must be null-only in TLS 1.3
truth: Set legacy_compression_methods to exactly one byte of value zero (null compression) in TLS 1.3 ClientHello; any other value must cause the server to abort with illegal_parameter.
quote: this vector MUST contain exactly one byte, set to zero

subject: DSA signature certificates for TLS
truth: At minimum, configure TLS servers with an RSA signature certificate or an ECDSA signature certificate; DSA, DH, and ECDH certificates are not commonly used and should be avoided.
quote: TLS servers conforming to this specification shall be configured with an RSA signature certificate or an ECDSA signature certificate.

subject: TLS 1.3 HelloRetryRequest key_share validation
truth: Upon receipt of key_share in HelloRetryRequest, the client MUST verify selected_group was in supported_groups and NOT in the original key_share, or abort with illegal_parameter.
quote: the client MUST verify that (1) the selected_group field corresponds to a group which was provided in the "supported_groups" extension in the original ClientHello and (2) the selected_group field does not correspond to a group which was provided in the "key_share" extension in the original ClientHello.

subject: Verifier impersonation resistance requires approved crypto with minimum 112-bit security strength
truth: Stop using weak or unapproved cryptographic algorithms for phishing resistance (the capability NIST called "verifier impersonation resistance" in the now-withdrawn SP 800-63B). Per the current standard, NIST SP 800-63B-4, use approved cryptographic algorithms whose keys provide at least the minimum security strength specified in the latest revision of SP 800-131A (112-bit as of this publication).
quote: Approved cryptographic algorithms SHALL be used to establish verifier impersonation resistance
_audit_fix: STALE: Cites NIST SP 800-63B, withdrawn 2025-08-01 and superseded by SP 800-63B-4; should be re-anchored to the current revision even though the 112-bit/SP 800-131A cr

subject: Key provisioning requires authenticated protected channels
truth: truth (unchanged, still valid): "Use authenticated protected channels (e.g., a mutually authenticated TLS session) or in-person procedures for key provisioning to protect against man-in-the-middle attacks — do not provision keys over unprotected channels." Update metadata: source = "NIST SP 800-63B-4", version = "NIST SP 800-63B-4 (Final, July 2025) — Digital Identity Guidelines: Authentication and Authenticator Management, Sec. 4.1 authenticator binding / key establishment requirements"; drop the withdrawn "NIST SP 800-63B" citation. Re-verify/re-ground the `quote` field against the actual SP 800-63B-4 text (e.g., the key-establishment/authenticator-binding clause requiring a mutually authenticated protected channel) since the original verbatim quote came from the withdrawn document and may not appear verbatim in -4.
quote: protocols for key provisioning SHALL use authenticated protected channels or be performed in person
_audit_fix: STALE: Cites NIST SP 800-63B, withdrawn 2025-08-01 and superseded by SP 800-63B-4; the withdrawn document should not be presented as the current authoritative source.

subject: cookies lack confidentiality/integrity by default even with HTTPS
truth: Do not rely on cookies alone for confidentiality or integrity; cookies do not provide confidentiality or integrity from network attackers by default, even when used with HTTPS.
quote: by default, cookies do not provide confidentiality or integrity from network attackers, even when used in conjunction with HTTPS

subject: Hybrid symmetric-asymmetric approach
truth: Use asymmetric-key algorithms only for digital signatures and initial key establishment; use symmetric-key algorithms for all other encryption, especially bulk data protection.
quote: a hybrid approach is often used whereby asymmetric-key algorithms are used for the generation and verification of digital signatures and for initial key establishment, while symmetric-key algorithms are used for all other purposes

subject: Password length > algorithm block size gets hashed first
truth: Stop assuming longer passwords always increase security; passwords larger than an algorithm-specific size are first hashed, reducing effective search space to the hash length.
quote: passwords larger than a certain algorithm-specific size are first hashed, which reduces an attacker's effective search space to the length of the hash algorithm

subject: auditing for key compromise prevention
truth: Stop relying on key management without auditing; perform compliance audits, cryptographic mechanism audits, and human-action audits to detect and recover from key compromises.
quote: Auditing is a mechanism used for the prevention, detection, and recovery from key compromises

subject: Verifier compromise resistant public keys require approved crypto at 112-bit minimum
truth: Stop storing verifier compromise resistant secrets without approved hash algorithms; public keys must use approved crypto at minimum 112-bit security strength per SP 800-131A.
quote: public keys stored by the verifier SHALL be associated with the use of approved cryptographic algorithms and SHALL provide at least the minimum security strength specified in the latest revision of SP 800-131A

subject: TLS server certificate Authority Information Access
truth: Require both id-ad-caIssuers (HTTP URL for issuing CA certificates) and id-ad-ocsp (HTTP URL for the issuing CA OCSP responder) in the Authority Information Access extension.
quote: id-ad-caIssuers Required. Access method entry contains HTTP URL for certificates issued to issuing CA

subject: Perfect forward secrecy in TLS cipher suites
truth: Prefer cipher suites using ephemeral DH or ephemeral ECDH (DHE or ECDHE) to provide perfect forward secrecy; each ephemeral key-pair shall have at least 112 bits of security.
quote: Cipher suites using ephemeral DH and ephemeral ECDH (i.e., those with DHE or ECDHE in the second mnemonic) provide perfect forward secrecy

subject: Mandatory TLS extensions for clients
truth: Clients shall be configured to use Renegotiation Indication, Server Name Indication, Extended Master Secret, Signature Algorithms, and Certificate Status Request extensions.
quote: The client shall be configured to use the following extensions

subject: CSP must offer non-RESTRICTED authenticator alternative
truth: When accepting a RESTRICTED authenticator, offer subscribers at least one alternate authenticator that is not RESTRICTED and can be used to authenticate at the required AAL.
quote: Offer subscribers at least one alternate authenticator that is not RESTRICTED

subject: password special character restrictions
truth: Stop rejecting passwords with spaces and special characters; properly hashed passwords are not sent intact to databases, so SQL-injection-based restrictions are unnecessary.
quote: Many services reject passwords with spaces and various special characters

subject: Software cryptographic key storage
truth: The key SHALL be stored in suitably secure storage (e.g., keychain storage, TPM, or TEE) and SHALL be strongly protected against unauthorized disclosure by access controls.
quote: stored in suitably secure storage available to the authenticator application

subject: Cookie Path attribute does not provide integrity protection
truth: Do not rely on the Path attribute for integrity; mutually distrusting services on different paths of the same host must not use cookies for security-sensitive information.
quote: the Path attribute does not provide any integrity protection because the user agent will accept an arbitrary Path attribute in a Set-Cookie header

subject: Non-compliant DH/MQV finite field schemes after 2020
truth: Stop using any finite field DH or MQV scheme that does not conform to SP 800-56A; all non-compliant finite field DH and MQV schemes are disallowed after December 31, 2020.
quote: After December 31, 2020, the use of these schemes is disallowed

subject: CMAC with TDEA (two-key and three-key)
truth: Stop using two-key TDEA for CMAC generation (disallowed) and three-key TDEA for CMAC generation (deprecated through 2023, disallowed after 2023); use AES for CMAC instead.
quote: Two-key TDEA Disallowed

subject: Unapproved cryptographic algorithms
truth: Only use cryptographic algorithms approved by inclusion in a FIPS or NIST Special Publication; unapproved algorithms must not be used for protecting sensitive information.
quote: an approved algorithm must be used; approval is indicated by inclusion in a FIPS or SP

subject: SHA-1 in TLS 1.3 CertificateVerify
truth: Stop using SHA-1 in TLS 1.3 CertificateVerify signatures; use only algorithms from the signature_algorithms extension that are compatible with the end-entity certificate.
quote: The SHA-1 algorithm MUST NOT be used in any signatures of CertificateVerify messages.

subject: TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 cipher suite
truth: Stop using TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256; it uses RSA key exchange (static RSA) which provides no forward secrecy and is deprecated per SP 800-131A transition guidance.
quote: TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256

subject: SWF and CSS mixed content scripting
truth: Do not embed SWF movies or CSS over insecure connections because both can script the embedding page, and some browsers do not issue mixed content warnings for SWF files.
quote: both cascading style sheets and SWF movies can script the embedding page

subject: TLS 1.3 forbids renegotiation
truth: Do not implement TLS renegotiation when using TLS 1.3; terminate the connection with an unexpected_message alert if a ClientHello is received after handshake completion.
quote: TLS 1.3 forbids renegotiation

subject: TLS exporter context handling
truth: In TLS 1.3, providing no context and providing an empty context compute the same exporter value — unlike in previous TLS versions where they produced different outputs.
quote: providing no context computes the same value as providing an empty context

subject: Pre-shared key establishment
truth: Establish pre-shared keys only via a secure method (e.g., secure manual distribution or automated key-establishment scheme), never by transmitting the key in the clear.
quote: A secret key that has previously been established between the parties who are authorized to use it by means of some secure method

subject: ECDSA signature validation must check exact signature length
truth: When validating ECDSA signatures in JWS, first check that the JWS Signature value is exactly the expected length (64, 96, or 132 octets) before splitting into R and S.
quote: MUST be a 64-octet sequence. If it is not a 64-octet sequence, the validation has failed.

subject: JWT 'none' algorithm
truth: Do not accept or generate JWTs with algorithm 'none' unless the JWT is cryptographically protected by a separate transport layer and the caller explicitly requests it.
quote: using "none" unless explicitly requested to do so by the caller.

subject: Algorithm selection when none is specified
truth: When developing specifications, cryptographic algorithms specified in FIPS and SPs must be used when available; do not default to unapproved or proprietary algorithms.
quote: cryptographic algorithms specified in FIPS and SPs must be used, when available

subject: Digital signature assurance requirements
truth: Do not accept digital signatures without verifying domain parameter validity, public key validity, proof of private key possession, and identity of the key-pair owner.
quote: assurance of domain parameter validity, assurance of public key validity, assurance that the key-pair owner actually possesses the private key, and assurance of the identity of the key-pair owner

subject: Digital signature minimum security strength for attestation
truth: Stop using digital signatures weaker than 112 bits of security strength for attestation; use algorithms meeting the minimum security strength specified in SP 800-131A.
quote: signed using a digital signature that provides at least the minimum security strength specified in the latest revision of SP 800-131A (112 bits as of the date of this publication)

subject: Server must validate ticket age for 0-RTT PSK
truth: When accepting 0-RTT via a PSK from a NewSessionTicket, the server MUST validate the ticket age against a small tolerance and reject 0-RTT if the age is out of range.
quote: MUST validate that the ticket age for the selected PSK identity

subject: Missing reauthentication timeout for extended sessions
truth: Stop allowing indefinite sessions; cap reauthentication per NIST SP 800-63B assurance level, never by a single number: AAL1 — overall reauthentication timeout SHOULD be no more than 30 days (inactivity timeout optional); AAL2 — overall timeout SHOULD be no more than 24 hours, inactivity timeout SHOULD be no more than 1 hour; AAL3 — overall timeout SHALL be no more than 12 hours, inactivity timeout SHOULD be no more than 15 minutes. The session SHOULD be terminated when the applicable limit is reached.
quote: SHOULD be repeated at least once per 30 days
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: The 30-day reauthentication ceiling in NIST SP 800-63B applies only to AAL1; AAL2 requires reauthentication at least every 24 hours and AAL3 at least every 12 h

subject: Cryptographic key naming for user recognition
truth: Give cryptographic keys appropriately descriptive names that are meaningful to users so they can recognize and recall which key to use for which authentication task.
quote: Give cryptographic keys appropriately descriptive names that are meaningful to users

subject: TLS 1.3 multiple ticket independence
truth: Use distinct keys for each ticket established on the same connection; compromise of one ticket's PSK must not compromise connections established with other tickets.
quote: if multiple tickets are established on the same connection, they are associated with different keys, so compromise of the PSK associated with one ticket does not lead to the compromise of connections established with PSKs associated with other tickets

subject: 415 response must include Accept-Encoding for coding failures
truth: When failing a request due to unsupported content coding, respond with 415 and include Accept-Encoding; for non-coding 415 errors, MUST NOT include Accept-Encoding.
quote: Servers that fail a request due to an unsupported content coding ought to respond with a 415 (Unsupported Media Type) status and include an Accept-Encoding header field

subject: NIST SP 800-175Br1 references approved crypto standards
truth: Use only NIST-approved cryptographic algorithms (AES, SHA-2/3, ECDSA, RSA with OAEP) and avoid deprecated algorithms not listed in FIPS and SP 800-series standards.
quote: The cryptographic algorithms and schemes in FIPS and SPs are usually included in ISO/IEC standards

subject: RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 signature algorithms in TLS 1.3 handshake
truth: Do not use RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 signature algorithms for TLS 1.3 handshake signatures; they are only defined for certificates and backward compatibility with TLS 1.2.
quote: not defined for use in signed TLS handshake messages

subject: ClientHello legacy_version must be 0x0303 (TLS 1.2)
truth: Set the legacy_version field in ClientHello to 0x0303 (TLS 1.2), not to the highest supported version; version preferences go in the supported_versions extension.
quote: legacy_version field MUST be set to 0x0303

subject: Referer header stripping by intermediaries
truth: Intermediaries SHOULD NOT strip or modify the Referer header when the field value shares the same scheme and host as the target URI; this is a strong recommendation (not an absolute MUST NOT) since doing so can break CSRF protections that rely on Referer.
quote: An intermediary SHOULD NOT modify or delete the Referer header field when the field value shares the same scheme and host as the target URI.
_audit_fix: WRONG: RFC 9110 §10.1.3 (and the fact's own quote field) says an intermediary 'SHOULD NOT modify or delete' the Referer field in this case — a recommendation, not an a

subject: Max-Forwards header not checked/decremented by intermediaries
truth: Each intermediary that receives a TRACE or OPTIONS request containing a Max-Forwards header field MUST check and update its value prior to forwarding the request.
quote: MUST check and update its value prior to forwarding the request

subject: Key usage restriction
truth: Stop using a key for purposes other than its designated use; a key designated for key transport must not be used for digital signature generation or verification.
quote: a key designated for key transport must not be used for the generation or verification of digital signatures

subject: exportable cryptographic keys
truth: Stop allowing secret keys to be exportable from cryptographic devices; secret keys SHALL NOT be exportable from single-factor cryptographic device authenticators.
quote: SHALL NOT be exportable (i.e., cannot be removed from the device)

subject: Digital signature without hashing
truth: Always hash the data using an approved hash function before generating or verifying a digital signature; the signature is computed on the hash, not the raw data.
quote: obtain a hash value, which is a condensed version of the data to be signed (i.e.,

subject: OAuth client redirect validation
truth: Do not blindly redirect to any URL passed as a parameter; only redirect if the target URLs are allowed or the request origin and integrity can be authenticated.
quote: clients should only redirect if the target URLs are allowed or if the origin and integrity of a request can be authenticated

subject: source authentication for automated key establishment
truth: When using automated key establishment methods, require source authentication for participating entities via a PKI or a manually distributed authentication key.
quote: source authentication is required for the participating entities

subject: ECDSA signature R/S octet encoding must not strip leading zero octets
truth: When encoding ECDSA signature values R and S as octet sequences, do NOT strip leading zero octets; each must be exactly the full length (32, 48, or 66 octets).
quote: MUST NOT be shortened to omit any leading zero octets

subject: CORS at the authorization endpoint
truth: CORS MUST NOT be supported at the authorization endpoint because the client does not access this endpoint directly; the client redirects the user agent to it.
quote: CORS MUST NOT be supported at the authorization endpoint

subject: Out-of-band authentication secret entropy
truth: Generate random authentication secrets with at least 20 bits of entropy using an approved random bit generator; if less than 64 bits, implement rate-limiting.
quote: at least 20 bits of entropy

subject: PKCE downgrade attack prevention
truth: Authorization servers MUST reject token requests with code_verifier if no code_challenge was in the authorization request, to prevent PKCE downgrade attacks.
quote: MUST mitigate PKCE downgrade attacks

subject: DSA domain parameters
truth: Do not reuse DSA domain parameters across a domain of users without careful management; domain parameters must be generated and validated per NIST standards.
quote: Domain parameters The parameters used with a cryptographic algorithm that are common to a domain of users.

subject: OTP authenticator output entropy
truth: Do not truncate OTP authenticator output below 6 decimal digits (20 bits of entropy); use an approved block cipher or hash function to combine key and nonce.
quote: truncated to as few as 6 decimal digits (approximately 20 bits of entropy)

subject: backup authenticator for reporting loss
truth: Do provide a backup or alternate authenticator (memorized secret or physical) so subscribers can securely report loss without needing the lost authenticator.
quote: provide the subscriber with a method of authenticating to the CSP using a backup or alternate authenticator

subject: Legacy symmetric encryption algorithms (non-AEAD)

subject: 3DES/TDEA cipher suites in TLS
truth: Stop using 3DES/TDEA cipher suites in TLS; they are no longer allowed due to limited data that can be processed under a single key.
quote: Cipher suites that use the Triple Data Encryption Aglorithm (TDEA, also written as 3DES) are no longer allowed

subject: PBKDF2 iteration count too low
truth: Stop using low iteration counts for PBKDF2; use at least 10,000 iterations, as large as verification server performance will allow.
quote: at least 10,000 iterations

subject: storing password hash without a pepper (secret salt)
truth: Perform an additional iteration of a key derivation function using a secret salt value stored separately from the hashed passwords.

truth: Reject any cipher not on the permitted list; RC4, 3DES, DES, export, NULL, anonymous, and CBC-only legacy ciphers must be disabled.

truth: Stop relying on cookies as ambient authority for authentication; they are vulnerable to cross-site request forgery (CSRF) attacks.

subject: Elliptic curve key agreement with len(n) < 224
truth: Stop using elliptic curve key agreement schemes where the curve order n has length less than 224 bits; len(n) < 224 is disallowed.
quote: len(n) < 224 Disallowed

truth: Stop using three-key TDEA in a CMAC-based KDF; it is disallowed after December 31, 2023. Use AES-128, AES-192, or AES-256 instead.

subject: TLS server certificate key usage
truth: Require ECDH or DH in the keyAgreement field of the Key Usage extension for TLS server certificates; prohibit anyExtendedKeyUsage.
quote: Prohibited: anyExtendedKeyUsage

subject: Extended Master Secret extension (RFC 7627)
truth: Enable the Extended Master Secret extension (RFC 7627) on servers supporting TLS 1.0–1.2 to prevent triple-handshake MITM attacks.

subject: OTP rate limiting for low-entropy authenticators
truth: Implement rate-limiting that limits failed authentication attempts when the authenticator output has less than 64 bits of entropy.
quote: If the authenticator output has less than 64 bits of entropy, the verifier SHALL implement a rate-

subject: OTP collection channel security
truth: Stop collecting OTPs over unencrypted or unauthenticated channels; use approved encryption and an authenticated protected channel.
quote: SHALL use approved encryption and an authenticated protected channel when collecting the OTP

subject: forgotten memorized secret recovery
truth: Do not issue additional 'backup' memorized secrets; use biometric or two physical authenticators plus a confirmation code instead.
quote: Additional “backup” memorized secrets do not mitigate this because they are just as

subject: TLS 1.3 exporter PRF construction
truth: TLS 1.3 replaces the TLS PRF with HKDF for exporter computation; implementations must use the new HKDF-Expand-Label construction.
quote: This document replaces the PRF with HKDF, thus requiring a new construction

subject: open redirectors
truth: Clients and authorization servers MUST NOT expose open redirectors that forward browsers to arbitrary URIs from query parameters.
quote: MUST NOT expose URLs that forward the user's browser to arbitrary URIs obtained from a query parameter

subject: Message Authentication Code (MAC) definition
truth: Use only NIST-approved MAC algorithms (e.g., HMAC, CMAC) with a symmetric key; never use a non-approved or keyless hash as a MAC.
quote: Message Authentication Code (MAC) A cryptographic checksum on data that uses an approved security function and a symmetric key

subject: NIST crypto standards reference latest revision
truth: Always refer to the latest officially approved revision of a FIPS or SP; do not rely on the revision number in the document body.
quote: the reader must refer to the latest version of the FIPS or SP that has been officially approved

subject: Password hints and knowledge-based questions
truth: Stop allowing password hints and stop prompting users with security questions (e.g., 'first pet's name') when choosing passwords.

associative: what was the name of your first pet
what is your mother's maiden name

subject: rate limiting consecutive failed authentication attempts
truth: Stop allowing unlimited login attempts; limit consecutive failed authentication attempts on a single account to no more than 100.
quote: the verifier SHALL limit consecutive failed authentication attempts on a single account to no more than 100

subject: session secret generation origin
truth: The session secret MUST be generated by the session host (server), not the client, in direct response to an authentication event.
quote: generated by the session host during an interaction, typically immediately following authentication

subject: JWS/JWE algorithm requirement levels
truth: Algorithm implementation requirements for JWS and JWE must be one of Required, Recommended, Optional, Deprecated, or Prohibited.
quote: The algorithm implementation requirements for JWS and JWE, which must be one the words Required, Recommended, Optional, Deprecated, or Prohibited.

subject: RSA signature algorithm selection in TLS 1.3
truth: Prefer RSASSA-PSS (rsa_pss_rsae_*) over RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 for TLS 1.3 handshake signatures; PKCS1-v1_5 is only for certificates.
quote: RSASSA-PSS RSAE algorithms

subject: JWT key-algorithm binding
truth: Stop using the same key across different algorithms; bind each key to exactly one algorithm and enforce it at verification time.
quote: each key MUST be used with exactly one algorithm, and this MUST be checked when the cryptographic operation is performed

subject: DSA key lengths below 2048-bit L
truth: Stop using DSA with (L, N) less than (2048, 224) for signature generation; use (2048, 224), (2048, 256), or (3072, 256) instead.

subject: 3TDEA three-key requirement and 2023 deadline
truth: If using TDEA, only use three distinct keys; after December 31, 2023, 3TDEA is disallowed for applying cryptographic protection.
quote: After December 31, 2023, 3TDEA will be disallowed for applying cryptographic protection

subject: Client certificate policy checking
truth: The server shall check certificate policies and perform full certification path validation per RFC 5280 for client certificates.
quote: The server shall be able to determine the certificate policies that the client certificate is trusted for

subject: Encrypt-then-MAC extension for CBC mode
truth: The Encrypt-then-MAC extension shall be supported when CBC mode cipher suites are configured, to prevent padding oracle attacks.
quote: The Encrypt-then-MAC extension shall be supported when CBC mode cipher suites are configured

subject: OCSP for TLS server certificates
truth: Require that status information for TLS server certificates be made available via the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP).
quote: status information for TLS server certificates is required to be made available via the Online Certificate Status Protocol

subject: OTP collection over authenticated protected channel
truth: Use approved encryption and an authenticated protected channel when collecting the OTP to resist eavesdropping and MitM attacks.
quote: use approved encryption and an authenticated protected channel when collecting the OTP

subject: minimum security strength for cryptographic authenticators
truth: Stop using keys and algorithms below 112 bits of security strength; use only approved cryptography meeting SP 800-131A minimums.
quote: SHALL provide at least the minimum security length specified in the latest revision of SP 800-131A (112 bits as of the date of this publication)

subject: Cookie Domain attribute
truth: Do not set a Domain attribute on cookies; omitting Domain restricts the cookie to the origin server only, which is more secure.
quote: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT",

subject: persistent cookie storage control
truth: When users prevent persistent storage, user agents MUST treat all received cookies as if the persistent-flag were set to false.
quote: MUST treat all received cookies as if the persistent-flag were set to false

subject: CR and NUL in field values
truth: Reject or map CR and NUL characters in field values to SP, and strip leading/trailing whitespace before consuming field values.
quote: CR and NUL in field values are to be rejected or mapped to SP, and leading and trailing whitespace needs to be stripped from field values before they are consumed

subject: ECDSA curve with n < 224 bits for signing
truth: Stop using ECDSA curves where the order n has length less than 224 bits for signature generation; use curves with len(n) ≥ 224.
quote: ECDSA: len(n) < 224

subject: Use of unvalidated cryptographic modules
truth: Stop using cryptographic algorithms or modules that have not been validated; use only FIPS 140 validated cryptographic modules.
quote: Use Validated Algorithms and Cryptographic Modules

subject: Private key secrecy and exclusive use
truth: Do not share or reuse private keys across entities; a private key must be kept secret and used only by the entity that owns it.
quote: a private key must be kept secret and used only by the entity that owns and is authorized to use the private key

subject: CA signature algorithm strength
truth: Stop using SHA-1 or weaker signature algorithms; require sha256WithRSAEncryption or stronger for CA certificates with RSA keys.
quote: sha256WithRSAEncryption {1 2 840 113549 1 1 11}, or stronger

subject: Renegotiation Indication extension (RFC 5746)
truth: Enable the Renegotiation Indication extension (RFC 5746) on servers supporting TLS 1.0–1.2 to prevent session splicing attacks.
quote: Server implementations shall perform initial and subsequent renegotiations in accordance with RFC 5746

subject: Client Certificate URL extension
truth: Do not support the Client Certificate URL TLS extension; it can be used for denial of service attacks against innocent servers.
quote: If the client certificate URL extension is supported, the server shall be configured

subject: JWT algorithm enforcement
truth: Do not accept JWT algorithm from the token header; validate that the algorithm used is in an application-controlled allowlist.
quote: it is an application decision which algorithms may be used in a given context

subject: AES-GCM cipher suites for TLS
truth: Use only NIST-approved cryptographic algorithms; AES-GCM (RFC 5288) is an approved authenticated encryption mechanism for TLS.
quote: AES Galois Counter Mode (GCM) Cipher Suites for TLS

subject: Elliptic curve selection for TLS certificates
truth: Stop using curves other than P-256 or P-384; use only NIST SP 800-186 named curves P-256 or P-384 for ECDSA/ECDH certificates.
quote: The curve should be P-256 or P-384

subject: Look-up secret reuse prevention
truth: A given secret from a look-up authenticator SHALL be used successfully only once; verifiers must enforce single-use semantics.
quote: A given secret from an authenticator SHALL be used successfully only once

subject: session fixation via cookies
truth: Do not store session identifiers in cookies without regeneration on login; doing so creates session fixation vulnerabilities.
quote: when storing session identifiers in cookies, developers often create session fixation vulnerabilities

subject: Cookies lack scheme isolation
truth: Do not assume cookies are isolated by scheme; cookies for a given host may be available to other schemes like ftp and gopher.
quote: Cookies do not provide isolation by scheme

subject: HSTS URI loading must upgrade http to https
truth: When loading an 'http' URI that matches a Known HSTS Host, the UA MUST replace the URI scheme with 'https' before proceeding.
quote: The UA MUST replace the URI scheme with "https" [RFC2818], and

subject: PBES2 password length bounds
truth: Use passwords no shorter than 16 octets (PBES2-HS256+A128KW) or 32 octets (PBES2-HS512+A256KW) and no longer than 128 octets.
quote: no shorter than 16 octets and no longer than 128 octets

subject: Asymmetric encryption uses public key, not private key
truth: For asymmetric encryption, encrypt with the public key and decrypt with the private key; do not encrypt with the private key.
quote: the encryption is performed using the public key, and the decryption is performed using the private key

subject: RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 for digital signatures
truth: RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 remains NIST-approved for digital signature generation under FIPS 186-5 (subject to stated constraints: approved hash functions only, and RSA key pairs generated per FIPS 186-5 Appendix B.3). RSA PKCS#1 v2.x (PSS) is the preferred choice for new designs for its stronger security proof, but v1.5 is not deprecated or prohibited — do not treat existing PKCS1-v1.5 signature code as non-compliant on that basis alone.
quote: algorithm as specified in RFC 801753 and PKCS 1 54 (version 1.5 and higher) and the

subject: TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are only conditionally approved
truth: Stop using TLS 1.0 and 1.1 by default; they are approved only when required for interoperability with non-government systems.
quote: TLS versions 1.1 and 1.0 are approved only when they are required for interoperability with non-government systems

subject: CBC cipher suites require Encrypt-then-MAC extension
truth: When negotiating CBC cipher suites, servers must support the Encrypt-then-MAC extension to mitigate MAC-then-encrypt attacks.
quote: Servers that are configured to negotiate CBC cipher suites shall support this extension

subject: OTP zeroization after use
truth: Stop retaining unencrypted OTP keys, activation secrets, or biometric samples after OTP generation; zeroize them immediately.
quote: SHALL be zeroized immediately after an OTP has been generated

subject: Single-factor software authenticator cloning prohibition
truth: Single-factor cryptographic software authenticators SHALL NOT facilitate the cloning of the secret key onto multiple devices.
quote: SHALL NOT facilitate the cloning of the secret key onto multiple devices

subject: Session Management
truth: Embed and verify a session identifier into web requests to prevent CSRF; do not rely on same-origin or referer headers alone.
quote: embed and verify a session identifier into web requests to prevent the ability for a valid URL or request to be unintentionally or maliciously activated

subject: TLS 1.3 legacy_compression_methods
truth: Set legacy_compression_methods to a single null byte (0) in ServerHello, and ClientHello must include only null compression.
quote: uint8 legacy_compression_method = 0;

subject: TDEA / Triple DES (3TDEA)
truth: Stop using TDEA/Triple DES for new applications; its security life is nearing conclusion and NIST is discontinuing approval.
quote: TDEA is nearin g its conclusion, so NIST announced plans

subject: Private key backup for digital signatures
truth: Do not encourage backup copies of private keys used for digital signatures; generate new key pairs and certificates instead.
quote: backup copies of the private key to be maintained for continuity of operations, but this is not encouraged

subject: Minimum security strength requirements by impact level
truth: Use at least 112-bit security strength for Low-impact, 128-bit for Moderate-impact, and 192-bit for High-impact information.
quote: SP 800-152 requires a security strength of at least 112 bits for the protection

subject: cryptographic key strength
truth: Stop using keys below 112 bits of security strength; use keys meeting at least the minimum security length from SP 800-131A.
quote: at least the minimum security length specified in the latest revision of SP 800-131A (112 bits as of the date of this publication)

subject: excessive password complexity requirements
truth: Stop requiring excessively long or complex memorized secrets; they are less memorable and more likely to be stored unsafely.
quote: Highly complex memorized secrets introduce a new potential vulnerability: they are less likely to be memorable, and it is more likely that they will be written down or stored electronically in an unsafe manner

subject: SHA-1 certificate signatures
truth: Do not use SHA-1 signatures for certificates; only ecdsa-with-SHA256, ecdsa-with-SHA384, or ecdsa-with-SHA512 are permitted.
quote: SHA-1 signatures are not permitted

subject: HMAC keys less than 112 bits
truth: Stop using HMAC keys less than 112 bits in length for MAC generation; use keys with at least 112 bits of security strength.
quote: The use of HMAC keys less than 112 bits in length is no longer allowed for the generation of a MAC

subject: Digital signature algorithms without approved cryptographic hash functions
truth: Do not use digital signature algorithms without an approved cryptographic hash function during generation and verification.
quote: NIST-approved digital signature algorithms require the use of an approved cryptographic hash function

subject: DSA (DSS) certificates
truth: Split into an accurate claim: "NIST SP 800-52r2 uses 'DSS' as a historical synonym for DSA in TLS cipher-suite names (quote: 'DSA is referred to as DSS (Digital Signature Standard) for historical reasons') — SP 800-52r2 itself still documents DSA as a supported certificate-signature option (alongside RSA/ECDSA) and does not ban it; a conforming TLS server need only have RSA or ECDSA. Avoid DSA/DSS certificates on separate, current grounds: FIPS 186-5 (effective 2023-02-03) removed DSA as an approved algorithm for generating NEW digital signatures (retaining it only to verify pre-existing legacy signatures), and NIST SP 800-131A Rev. 3 likewise retires DSA for signature generation — use RSA, ECDSA, or EdDSA for new certificates instead."
quote: DSA is referred to as DSS (Digital Signature Standard) for historical reasons

subject: JWE compression side-channel
truth: Stop compressing JWE payloads before encryption; compressed ciphertexts reveal plaintext content through size differences.
quote: such compressed data often reveals information about the plaintext

subject: OAuth response type selection
truth: Use the authorization code response type instead of response types that issue access tokens at the authorization endpoint.
quote: authorization code response type instead of response types that cause access token issuance at the authorization endpoint

subject: CBC mode without approved authentication
truth: Do not use CBC mode alone; use an authenticated encryption mode (e.g., GCM, CCM) or pair CBC with a separate approved MAC.

subject: ECB (Electronic Codebook) mode
truth: Do not use ECB mode for encryption; it is insecure because identical plaintext blocks produce identical ciphertext blocks.
quote: Electronic Codebook mode; specified in SP 800-38A

subject: Minimum 112-bit security for all TLS cryptography
truth: All TLS cryptography — certificates, keys, signatures, symmetric algorithms — shall provide at least 112 bits of security.
quote: provide at least 112 bits of security.

subject: HTTP trailer field generation
truth: Do not generate trailer fields that are necessary for the user agent to receive, because intermediaries may discard them.
quote: a server SHOULD NOT generate trailer fields that it believes are necessary for the user agent to receive

subject: Non-validated cryptographic implementations
truth: Only use implementations of approved algorithms that have been validated and included in validated cryptographic modules.
quote: federal agencies are required to use only implementations of these algorithms that have been validated

subject: RSA key transport and static DH key exchanges
truth: Stop using cipher suites with RSA key transport or static Diffie-Hellman key exchanges; use forward-secret key exchanges.
quote: (HKDF) [37], and the removal of cipher suites that use RSA key transport or static Diffie-

subject: OCSP revocation checking for TLS certificates
truth: TLS servers shall be configured with certificates issued by a CA that publishes revocation information in OCSP responses.
quote: TLS servers shall be configured with certificates issued by a CA that publishes revocation

subject: Certificate validation
truth: Validate the server certificate chain, including revocation status via CRL or OCSP, before establishing a TLS connection.
quote: deny the connection or accept a potentially revoked or compromised certificate

subject: Multi-factor cryptographic software authenticator cloning prevention
truth: Do not facilitate cloning of secret keys onto multiple devices; discourage it through secure storage and access controls.
quote: SHALL NOT facilitate the cloning of the secret key onto multiple devices

subject: Weak key sizes in certificate paths
truth: Enforce minimum key sizes: reject certification paths with keys or signatures weaker than 2048-bit RSA or 224-bit ECDSA.
quote: certification paths containing keys or signatures weaker than 2048-bit RSA or 224-bit ECDSA are not appropriate

subject: 0-RTT early data replay risks
truth: Do not assume 0-RTT early data is fresh; it is vulnerable to replay and must be treated as idempotent or safe-to-replay.
quote: 0-RTT messages sent in the first flight have the same (encrypted) content types

subject: SHA-1 digital signature generation disallowed
truth: SHA-1 is disallowed for digital signature generation except where NIST protocol-specific guidance explicitly permits it.
quote: SHA-1 may only be used for digital signature generation where specifically allowed by NIST protocol-specific guidance.

subject: Key copy accountability
truth: Stop making uncontrolled copies of keys; every extra copy must be accounted for and destroyed if the key is compromised.
quote: these extra copies need to be accounted for

subject: TLS 1.2 is the minimum approved version
truth: Use TLS 1.2 as the minimum protocol version; it is approved for protecting Federal information when properly configured.
quote: TLS 1.2 is approved for the protection of Federal information when properly configured

subject: TLS 1.3 record length limit
truth: Reject any record with fragment length exceeding 2^14 bytes by terminating the connection with a record_overflow alert.
quote: The length MUST NOT exceed 2^14 bytes

subject: RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 in TLS 1.3 CertificateVerify
truth: Stop using RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 for TLS 1.3 CertificateVerify signatures; RSA signatures MUST use an RSASSA-PSS algorithm.
quote: RSA signatures MUST use an RSASSA-PSS algorithm, regardless of whether RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 algorithms appear in "signature_algorithms".

subject: PKCE code challenge method S256
truth: Use S256 as the PKCE code challenge method; do not use 'plain' which exposes the verifier in the authorization request.
quote: S256 is the only such method

subject: SHA-1 for digital signature generation
truth: Stop using SHA-1 for digital signature generation except where specifically allowed by NIST protocol-specific guidance.
quote: SHA-1 may only be used for digital signature generation where specifically allowed by

subject: Private key backup for key establishment
truth: Do backup private key-establishment keys for possible recovery to ensure access to critical encrypted data is not lost.
quote: critical data is not lost, PKIs often backup the private key- establishment key for

subject: Trust anchor minimization
truth: Configure the server with only the minimum necessary trust anchors for client authentication, not the default full set.
quote: The server shall be configured only with trust anchors that the system owner trusts

subject: TLS server certificate DNS name check
truth: Terminate the TLS connection if the DNS name or IP address in the server certificate does not match the client request.
quote: The client shall terminate the TLS connection if the name check fails.

subject: OTP authenticator cloning
truth: Stop allowing OTP secret keys to be cloned onto multiple devices; software OTP generators SHALL NOT facilitate cloning.
quote: SHOULD discourage and SHALL NOT facilitate the cloning of the secret key onto multiple devices

subject: non-approved cryptography
truth: Stop using non-approved cryptographic algorithms; approved cryptography SHALL be used for all cryptographic operations.
quote: Approved cryptography SHALL be used

subject: expired authenticators
truth: Stop allowing expired authenticators to be usable for authentication; reject them and indicate expiration as the cause.
quote: SHALL NOT be usable for authentication

subject: Three-key TDEA encryption after 2023
truth: Stop using three-key TDEA for encryption in new applications; it is deprecated through 2023 and disallowed after 2023.
quote: three-key TDEA is deprecated through December 31, 2023

subject: EdDSA (Edwards-curve Digital Signature Algorithm)
truth: Prefer EdDSA (Edwards-curve DSA) over older signature schemes; it is now specified in the latest revision of FIPS 186.
quote: EdDSA which is specified in the latest revision of FIPS 186

subject: Early Data Indication (0-RTT) extension
truth: Do not process early data received in the ClientHello message; TLS does not protect early data against replay attacks.
quote: Servers should not process early data received in the ClientHello message

SHA-1 must not be used for CertificateVerify in TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3 -- RFC 8446 explicitly forbids it there.
quote: SHA-1 is allowed for generating digital signatures in the TLS CertificateVerify message

subject: PSK cipher suites with TLS 1.2 for government cross-organization communication
truth: Do not use PSK cipher suites with TLS 1.2 when a government client or server communicates with non-government systems.
quote: shall not be used with TLS 1.2 when a government client or server communicates with non-government systems

subject: Multi-factor cryptographic software authenticator key zeroization
truth: Zeroize unencrypted keys, activation secrets, and biometric samples immediately after each authentication transaction.
quote: SHALL be zeroized immediately after an authentication transaction has taken place

subject: password composition rules
truth: Stop requiring composition rules (uppercase, digits, symbols); they produce predictable passwords and frustrate users.
quote: composition rules are commonly used in an attempt to increase the difficulty of guessing user-chosen passwords. Research has shown, however, that users respond in very predictable ways

subject: HSTS non-80 explicit ports must be preserved
truth: If the URI contains an explicit port component that is not equal to '80', the port component value MUST be preserved.
quote: the port component value MUST be preserved

subject: CBC cipher suites in TLS
truth: Stop using CBC cipher suites; use AEAD cipher suites (GCM, CCM) instead to prevent padding oracle and timing attacks.
quote: CBC-based attacks can be prevented by using AEAD cipher suites (e.g., GCM, CCM)

subject: TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1
truth: Configure clients to use TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3; TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are only for legacy private-sector interoperability.
quote: The client shall be configured to use TLS 1.2 and should be configured to use TLS 1.3.

subject: minimum password length
truth: Require a minimum password length based on threat model; short passwords yield to brute force and dictionary attacks.
quote: Password length has been found to be a primary factor in characterizing password strength

subject: confirmation code random generation
truth: Generate confirmation codes using an approved random bit generator (SP 800-90A), not a weak PRNG like Math.random().
quote: generated by an approved random bit generator

subject: Static RSA and Diffie-Hellman cipher suites
truth: Do not use static RSA or static Diffie-Hellman cipher suites; they lack forward secrecy and are removed in TLS 1.3.
quote: Static RSA and Diffie-Hellman cipher suites have been removed

subject: Sender role conformance
truth: A sender MUST NOT generate protocol elements that do not match the grammar defined by the corresponding ABNF rules.
quote: A sender MUST NOT generate protocol elements that do not match the grammar defined by the corresponding ABNF rules

subject: Accept-Encoding empty header means no encoding wanted
truth: An empty Accept-Encoding header field means the user agent does NOT want any content coding, not 'accept anything'.
quote: An Accept-Encoding header field with a field value that is empty implies that the user agent does not want any content coding in response.

subject: OAuth implicit grant (response type token)
truth: Stop using the implicit grant (response type token); use the authorization code grant (response type code) instead.
quote: clients SHOULD NOT use the implicit grant (response type token)

subject: ephemeral key length checking
truth: The client shall check the server public key length when the server uses ephemeral keys for master secret creation.
quote: The client shall also check the server public key length if the server uses ephemeral keys

subject: Session reauthentication timeout at AAL2
truth: Reauthenticate the subscriber at least once per 12 hours during extended usage, and after 30 minutes of inactivity.
quote: authentication of the subscriber SHALL be repeated at least once per 12 hours

subject: Memorized Secret Storage
truth: Store memorized secrets in a salted, hashed form, including a keyed hash; never store plaintext or unsalted hashes.
quote: Store memorized secrets in a salted, hashed form, including a keyed hash.

subject: PBES2 iteration count too small
truth: Do not use a small iteration count for PBES2; dictionary attacks are feasible if the iteration count is too small.
quote: dictionary-based attacks if the iteration count is too small

subject: DES and TDEA (3DES)
truth: Stop using DES and TDEA (3DES); they have been successfully attacked and no longer provide the desired protection.
quote: DES and TDEA are examples of such algorithms

subject: Digital signature timestamping without trusted timestamp authority
truth: Do not rely on signing time embedded in a message without a Trusted Timestamp Authority or verifier-supplied data.
quote: provides no assurance that the private key was used to sign the message at that time unless the accuracy of the time can be trusted

subject: Malformed STS headers
truth: Ignore any STS header fields that do not conform to the grammar; do not partially parse or silently correct them.
quote: The UA MUST ignore any STS header fields not conforming to the grammar specified in Section 6.1

subject: ECDH key derivation without distinct PartyUInfo/PartyVInfo
truth: Stop using ECDH key agreement without distinct apu/apv values; the apu and apv values MUST be distinct when used.
quote: The "apu" and "apv" values

subject: TLS 1.3 key_share extension - unsupported groups
truth: Clients MUST NOT offer any KeyShareEntry values for groups not listed in the client's supported_groups extension.
quote: Clients MUST NOT offer any KeyShareEntry values for groups not listed in the client's "supported_groups" extension.

subject: PKCE enforcement
truth: Make PKCE mandatory for all OAuth flows; do not make PKCE optional based on presence of code_challenge parameter.
quote: authorization server that supports PKCE but does not make its use mandatory for all flows can be susceptible to a PKCE downgrade attack

subject: redirect URI matching
truth: Authorization servers MUST use exact string matching for redirect URI comparison, not pattern or prefix matching.
quote: MUST utilize exact string matching

subject: Usability as security-critical requirement
truth: Do not treat usability as secondary; poor usability causes coping mechanisms that degrade security effectiveness.
quote: poor usability often results in coping mechanisms and unintended work-arounds that can ultimately degrade the effectiveness of security controls.

subject: Authorization code invalidation after first use
truth: Authorization codes MUST be invalidated by the authorization server after their first use at the token endpoint.
quote: authorization codes MUST be invalidated by the authorization server after their first use at the token endpoint

subject: Non-SP 800-56A-compliant key-agreement schemes
truth: Non-SP 800-56A-compliant key-agreement schemes have a termination date; use only DH groups listed in SP 800-56A.
